input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-02T02:36:36.790Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which was removed in MSTest v4. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and provide the correct replacement.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is **`Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()`** (inline in the test body). Let me update your files:
data.reasoningText:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which has been available since earlier versions). Let me update both the test file and the csproj.

Actually, in MSTest v4, `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` was introduced. The older `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` also works but `ThrowsExactly` is the preferred new API. Let me use `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` which is the MSTest v4 recommended replacement.


Now I'll update the project file to MSTest v4 and refactor the tests to use the new assertion method.
